info.name: DMS Auth APIs
info.description:
Authentication APIs for the DMS platform.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Initial Value | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | `http://localhost:3001/api` | API base URL |
| `access_token` | — | Auto-populated from login/register response |
| `refresh_token` | — | Auto-populated from login response |
| `otp` | — | OTP received (manually set from DB or email) |

request.description:
Register a new company and user (self-onboarding).

Returns `nextStep: "VERIFY_EMAIL"` — an OTP is automatically sent to the registered email.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Registration successful. Please verify your email.",
  "companyId": "uuid",
  "userId": "uuid",
  "companyUserId": "uuid",
  "nextStep": "VERIFY_EMAIL"
}

response.name: Weak Password

request.description:
Authenticate with email or username.

On success, `access_token` and `refresh_token` are auto-saved as collection variables.

response.name: Success (Single Company)
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Login successful",
  "accessToken": "jwt...",
  "refreshToken": "jwt...",
  "expiresIn": 900,
  "user": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "email": "john@acme.com",
    "firstName": "John",
    "lastName": "Doe",
    "displayName": null
  },
  "companies": [
    {
      "companyId": "uuid",
      "companyCode": "CMP000001",
      "legalName": "Acme Corp",
      "displayName": "Acme Corp Pvt Ltd",
      "isDefault": true
    }
  ],
  "defaultCompany": {
    "companyId": "uuid",
    "companyCode": "CMP000001",
    "legalName": "Acme Corp",
    "displayName": "Acme Corp Pvt Ltd",
    "isDefault": true

  "requiresCompanySelection": false
}

response.name: Account Locked

query.description: Verification purpose (REGISTER, LOGIN, RESET_PASSWORD, CHANGE_EMAIL, INVITE)

request.description:
Send a verification OTP to the given email.

OTP is valid for 15 minutes, max 5 attempts, max 3 resends.

The OTP is stored as a SHA-256 hash — check the DB (`email_verification_tokens` table) to retrieve it during development.

response.name: Success

request.description: Resend a verification OTP (max 3 resends allowed). Invalidates the previous pending OTP.

response.name: Success
Too Many Resends

request.description:
Verify an email with the OTP received.

> **Note:** Set the `otp` collection variable manually after retrieving it from the `email_verification_tokens` table.
> ```sql
> SELECT otp FROM email_verification_tokens WHERE email = 'john@acme.com' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;
> ```
> The OTP is SHA-256 hashed in the DB — this is the raw 6-digit OTP sent to the user.

response.name: OTP Expired
Too Many Attempts

request.description:
Request a password reset OTP.

Always returns the same generic success message regardless of whether the email exists (to prevent email enumeration).

If the user exists and is eligible (ACTIVE, email verified, not deleted):
- Invalidates any previous pending reset tokens
- Generates a cryptographically secure 6-digit OTP (SHA-256 hashed)
- Stores it with `purpose: RESET_PASSWORD`, 10-min expiry, max 5 attempts
- Publishes `PasswordResetRequestedEvent`
- Creates an ActivityLog entry

OTP is stored hashed — check the `email_verification_tokens` table: `SELECT * FROM email_verification_tokens WHERE purpose = 'RESET_PASSWORD' ORDER BY created_at DESC;`

response.name: Success

request.description: Resend a password reset OTP (max 5 resends allowed). Invalidates the previous pending OTP.

response.name: Success
Too Many Resends

request.description:
Verify the password reset OTP.

- Validates OTP format, expiry, and attempt count
- Uses constant-time SHA-256 comparison
- On success: marks token as VERIFIED (required before calling reset-password)
- Publishes `PasswordResetVerifiedEvent`
- Creates an ActivityLog entry

> **Note:** Set the `otp` collection variable manually after retrieving it from the `email_verification_tokens` table.
> ```sql
> SELECT * FROM email_verification_tokens WHERE email = 'john@acme.com' AND purpose = 'RESET_PASSWORD' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;
> ```

response.name: OTP Expired
Too Many Attempts

request.description:
Reset the password using a VERIFIED OTP.

Prerequisites:
1. Call `/forgot-password` to get an OTP
2. Call `/verify-password-reset` to mark the token as VERIFIED
3. Call this endpoint within the 10-min expiry

What happens inside a Prisma transaction:
- Hashes the new password with bcrypt (12 rounds)
- Updates user: passwordHash, passwordChangedAt, passwordResetRequired=false, forcePasswordChange=false, refreshTokenVersion++
- Marks the token as USED
- If `logoutFromAllDevices=true`: revokes all active sessions

On success:
- Publishes `PasswordChangedEvent`
- Creates an ActivityLog entry

response.name: Weak Password
OTP Expired

request.description:
Returns the complete profile of the authenticated user.

Includes:
- User info (name, email, mobile, gender, DOB, etc.)
- Active company details
- Company membership info
- Roles
- Current session info

Requires `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` header.

The `access_token` collection variable is auto-populated after login.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "firstName": "John",
  "middleName": null,
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "displayName": "John Doe",

request.description:
Update the authenticated user's profile.

Only personal info fields can be updated:
- firstName, middleName, lastName, displayName
- mobileNumber, alternateMobileNumber
- gender, dateOfBirth, bloodGroup, maritalStatus, nationality

Email, username, and security fields cannot be updated via this endpoint.

Change the authenticated user's password.

Flow:
1. Validates current password
2. Validates password strength and confirmation
3. Hashes new password with bcrypt
4. In a Prisma transaction: updates password hash, increments refreshTokenVersion
5. If `logoutOtherDevices=true`: revokes all sessions except current
6. Publishes `PasswordChangedEvent`
7. Creates ActivityLog entry

After a successful password change, all existing JWTs are invalidated (refreshTokenVersion incremented).

response.name: Weak Password

formdata.description: Profile photo (JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP, max 5 MB)

request.description:
Upload a profile photo for the authenticated user.

Accepted formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP
Maximum size: 5 MB

Flow:
- Validates file type and size
- Stores file locally in `uploads/profiles/`
- Creates a `FileAttachment` record with `fileCategory: USER_PROFILE`
- Updates `User.profilePhotoUrl`
- Publishes `ProfilePhotoUploadedEvent`
- Creates ActivityLog entry

response.name: Invalid File Type
File Too Large

request.description:
Update the authenticated user's preferences.

- `preferredLanguage` and `timezone` are stored as direct User model fields
- `dateFormat`, `timeFormat`, and `theme` are stored in the User's `metadata` JSON field

Publishes `PreferencesUpdatedEvent` and creates ActivityLog entry.
Create a new user invitation. If the user already exists, creates CompanyUser (INVITED) + UserRole (source: INVITATION) records. Publishes UserInvitationCreatedEvent.
List all active invitations for the current user's company.
Get detailed information about a specific invitation.

Accept an invitation using the invitation token. Creates CompanyUser (ACTIVE) + UserRole records. If the user does not exist, creates a new user account (forcePasswordChange: true). Publishes UserInvitationAcceptedEvent.
Resend an invitation with a new token. Max 5 resends allowed. Publishes UserInvitationCreatedEvent.
Cancel a pending invitation. Cannot cancel already accepted invitations. Publishes UserInvitationCancelledEvent.
List roles for the active company with pagination, search, sorting, and filtering.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "New Role",
  "code": "NEW_ROLE",
  "displayName": "New Role Display",
  "description": "A new custom role",
  "parentRoleId": "optional-parent-uuid",
  "defaultLandingPage": "/dashboard",
  "defaultModule": "SALES"
}

request.description: Create a new custom role. Name and code must be unique within the company.
Update a role's display name, description, parent role, landing page, and module.
Change role status: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, or ARCHIVED.
Clone a role. Creates a copy with 'Copy' appended to the name. Does not clone users or permissions.
Soft delete a role. Cannot delete system roles, roles with active users, or the Company Admin role.
